name: Pickled Beet and Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off all but 2-inches of the beet tops, leaving the root ends attached.
2. Peel and slice the onions into 1/4-slices.
3. Heat enough water to cover beets until boiling.
4. Add beets and 2 tablespoons vinegar for each quart of water used.
5. Cover and bring to a boil. Cook until beets are tender, about 35 to 45 minutes.
6. Drain the beets, run cold water over them, slip off skins, and remove root ends.
7. Cut the beets into 1/4-inch slices.
8. Heat remaining ingredients (vinegar, sugar, pickling spices, salt, and water) until boiling in a 6-quart Dutch oven.
9. Reduce heat to simmer for 10 minutes, then stir in beet mixture.
10. Pack beet and onion pieces into hot jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
11. Bring syrup to a boil and pour over beets and onions, leaving about 1/2-inch headspace.
12. Seal the jars tightly.
13. Process filled jars in a boiling water bath for 30 minutes.
14. This yields approximately 8 pints.
NOTE: 7 cans (16 ounces each) of sliced beets can substitute the beets used in this recipe.
